topic: Someone finds out they have a half-sibling they never knew about
raw_output:
[story 1]

The envelope felt like a lead weight in my hand. Inside, a single, crisp photograph and a letter that made the blood pound in my temples. It was an undeniable document, a truth I hadn't been prepared to face. My jaw tightened so hard I thought my molars might crack. I paced the length of my cramped apartment, the floorboards groaning under my frantic steps. Every instinct screamed at me to throw the paper across the room, to shatter the evidence, but my hands were shaking too violently to move. I stared at the photo—a smiling face I didn't recognize, yet somehow, intrinsically mine. A low, guttural sound escaped my throat, raw and uncontrolled. I slammed my fist onto the nearest desk, the cheap wood protesting with a sharp crack. "No," I whispered, the sound barely audible over the roaring in my ears. "This isn't real."

[story 2]

I stood in the hallway, the silence of the house amplifying the frantic drumming in my chest. The confirmation sat on the kitchen counter, mocking me with its neat, official presentation. I picked it up, my knuckles white against the paper. A sudden, sharp intake of breath hitched in my throat, and I squeezed my eyes shut, fighting a desperate urge to vomit. When I opened them, the world seemed too bright, too sharp. I walked to the window, gripping the sill until my fingers ached, and stared out at the indifferent street. A deep, vibrating tension coiled beneath my skin, making my muscles rigid. I turned back to the counter, my voice coming out tight and brittle. "How could they keep this from me?" The sheer injustice of it churned a bitter acid in my stomach. I kicked a nearby chair over, the noise echoing the violent disturbance inside me.

[story 3]

It happened so fast. One minute I was sorting through old boxes, the next, the official paperwork was in my hands. A cold, hard realization settled over me, instantly freezing the warmth from my core. I dropped the papers. They fluttered to the floor like dead leaves, and I didn't bother to retrieve them. I spun around, my breath catching in a ragged gasp that felt too large for my lungs. My hands clenched into fists so tight I could feel the tendons strain in my forearms. I took two heavy strides toward the living room, my whole body vibrating with suppressed energy. I stopped abruptly, planting my feet wide, and let out a sharp, clipped sound of disbelief. "This is absurd," I managed, the words laced with venom. I started pacing again, faster this time, a caged animal desperate to break free of the confines of this unbelievable discovery.
